SEARCH
Package org.javalabs.jpa.query

Class Criteria

java.lang.Object
org.javalabs.jpa.query.Criteria
public class Criteria extends Object
JPA Criteria query.

A query criterion is an expression that jpa compares to query field values to determine whether to include the record that contains each value. For example, location = 'NY' is an expression that jpa can compare to values in a text field in a pooledBuffer. Another example could be, dept IN ('HR', 'RAndD'). Criteria queries are written using Java programming language APIs, are typesafe, and are portable. Such queries work regardless of the underlying data store. Example 1: Simple select query. 

 
 
 Criteria criteria = new Criteria()
     .select("emp_id", "emp_name", "location")
     .from("employees");

Example 2: Select query with conditions. 
 
 
 Criteria criteria = new Criteria()
     .select("emp_id", "emp_name", "location")
     .from("employees")
     .where("emp_mame").like("%John%")
     .and("location").in(Arrays.asList("NY", "LN"));

Example 3: Select query with order by. 
 
 
 Criteria criteria = new Criteria()
     .select("emp_id", "emp_name", "location")
     .from("employees")
     .where("emp_mame").like("%John%")
     .orderBy("location")
     .desc();

Example 4: Select query with group by. 
 
 
 Criteria criteria = new Criteria()
     .select("department", "AVG(salary)")
     .from("employees")
     .where("location").eq("NY")
     .groupBy("department")
     .having("AVG(salary)").gt(20000)
     .orderBy("AVG(salary)")
     .desc();
Author:
Sudiptasish Chanda
